raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this formal complaint against American Express for their gross negligence in handling a massive identity theft and credit card fraud case. Not only did the bank fail to protect me from over {$30000.00} in unauthorized charges, but they also summarily canceled my account despite overwhelming evidence that I was a victim of fraud. 

I have been a permanent resident of XXXX for the past XXXX years since XXXX XXXX. I was physically present in XXXX during all the transaction dates listed below. I did not authorize these charges, nor was I in possession of the physical card in the United States during this period. 

The fraudulent activity occurred across the XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) while I was thousands of miles away in XXXXXXXX XXXX unauthorized transactions include : {$7900.00} XXXX * XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$7400.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$2300.00}, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$1300.00} XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$2000.00} XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$450.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$200.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$3100.00} XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$150.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  {$93.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$490.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  {$1700.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX {$270.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX and more... 

I promptly disputed these charges and provided evidence of my residency and physical presence in XXXX. I was only in XXXX twice for short period in the past XXXX and XXXX years, for a wedding in XXXX in XXXX and for family in XXXX in XXXX. Both were far after the purhcase happened There was even suspect 's name appreared in XXXX XXXX XXXX transaction, which is totally not mine. However, American Express didn't investigate that suspect for payments, but dismissed my claim without providing any factual basis or evidence ( such as signed receipts or surveillance footage ) that would link me to these XXXX physical transactions. 

Following their flawed investigation, American Express unilaterally canceled my account, citing the balance caused by these fraudulent charges. By doing so, the bank has : XXXX. Violated the XXXX XXXX Billing Act ( FCBA ) by holding me liable for more than {$50.00} in unauthorized charges. 
XXXX. Failed to perform a " reasonable investigation '' as required by federal law. 
XXXX. Caused severe, unwarranted damage to my credit profile by reporting this fraud-induced balance as a consumer default/charge-off.
